summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on November 28, 2023. Key actions included approving payment of bills, adopting updates to the policies and procedure manual (including sick leave donation and medical opt-out policies), approving the transfer of $1 million into a savings account for interest earnings, approving a job description for a new position, appointing Steve Pegram as Chief Emeritus volunteer, and passing a resolution to proceed with a 4.5 mil replacement EMS tax levy. The board also entered executive session regarding personnel matters and heard public comment on a property/development concern and questions about the levy.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Nov 14 and Nov 28, 2023) – passed
Motion to adopt updates to policies and procedure manual (5 policy revisions including motor vehicle compliance, medical opt-out compensation, sick leave donation policy) – passed
Motion to transfer $1 million into savings account – passed
Motion to approve resolution R023-2023 to proceed with 4.5 mil replacement EMS tax levy – passed
Motion to approve job description for new position – passed
Motion to approve appointment of Steve Pegram as Chief Emeritus volunteer effective 11/29/23 – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:35 PM per ORC 121.22 – passed
Motion to adjourn regular meeting at 7:55 PM – passed
action_items: Mr. Rose to contact Don Cornwell (property owner at 6444 Charles Snyder Rd) to set up a meeting with the zoning inspector and fire chief regarding development/fire lane concerns
Fiscal officer to execute transfer of $1 million to savings account
Fiscal officer to report back after March bank reconciliation on potential adjustment to savings account amount
Prosecutor's office to finalize EMS levy documentation for Board signature
Michelle Dinwiddie / CPAAA Light Up Goshen tree fundraiser – last day to sign up is November 29
key_amounts: $1 million to be transferred into township savings account
4.5 mil replacement EMS tax levy (fire and emergency medical services)
$100 per tree for Light Up Goshen fundraiser
Approximately $150 more per year estimated increase for replacement levy
Lowest general fund balances by year: 2019 - $2.4M, 2020 - $2.3M, 2021 - $2.5M, 2022 - $3.2M, 2023 - $3.9M
$1.4 million minimum balance referenced after savings transfer
notable_topics: Public comment on property development and fire lane concerns near Charles Snyder Road
Replacement EMS levy questions and resolution (4.5 mil)
Policy and procedure manual updates (motor vehicle compliance, medical opt-out, sick leave donation)
Transfer of $1 million to savings account for interest earnings
Appointment of Steve Pegram as Chief Emeritus volunteer
Moment of remembrance for Fayette County fire chief and captain (two line-of-duty deaths)
notes: The transcript has significant transcription errors and repeated filler text ('Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.') throughout, making portions difficult to interpret. The invocation included a tribute to two Fayette County firefighters who died in the line of duty over the preceding weekend. An executive session was held from approximately 6:35 PM to 7:52 PM. The EMS levy resolution (R023-2023) was initially tabled and then brought back for a vote under matters before the board.
title: BOT 11-28-23
